scope.summary:
PROOF MT (no app code changes; proof only). GOAL: author and execute a runtime scenario suite that proves the three 'other-pillar' interop edges of the native editors — Stage (Pillar 17), Calendar (Pillar 2), and Locus (Pillar 6) — actually work end-to-end against a REAL PostgreSQL database and the REAL EventLedger / Flight Recorder (NO mocks, NO SQLite, NO in-memory substitute for the persistence layer). This MT mirrors the MT-046 InterconnectionScenarioProofs pattern exactly: each scenario crosses a surface boundary, drives real state through existing handshake_core HTTP APIs, and is additionally reachable purely via AccessKit ids so an out-of-process swarm agent can drive it without screen-reading. The MT proves these force-multiplier edges instead of merely asserting them; if any backend API or AccessKit id needed by a scenario does not yet exist, the scenario is recorded as a TYPED BLOCKER in the manifest (status='BLOCKED', blocker='<reason>') rather than mocked, stubbed, or silently skipped.

WHY THIS IS A PROOF-ONLY MT: This task creates NO new product/app code under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/**. It exercises surfaces, panes, commands, and AccessKit ids that earlier MTs already built (Stage pane = MT-068, Calendar binding = MT-067, Locus cross-reference = MT-066, EditorActions AccessKit registry = MT-041, KnowledgeGraph AccessKit = MT-042). The only files created are test files plus a manifest under src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/. If a needed pane/command/AccessKit id is missing because an upstream MT did not register it, the correct outcome is a typed blocker, not new app code (a backend rewrite is also forbidden — bind existing handshake_core APIs only).

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: Creates src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_other_pillar_interop_proofs.rs — a single Rust integration test file (harness = true) containing the four proof scenarios OP-01..OP-04 below, plus a sibling manifest src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/other_pillar_interop_manifest.json with one entry per scenario. Registers the suite under [[test]] in src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml ( name = 'test_other_pillar_interop_proofs', harness = true ). No other files change.

PERSISTENCE INVARIANT (HARD): Every scenario must run against a real PostgreSQL instance provisioned by the same workspace/PG fixture builder used in MT-044/MT-045/MT-046 (reuse it; do not invent a parallel fixture). The EventLedger / Flight Recorder assertions must read the real ledger via GET /api/flight_recorder (or GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/events, whichever route exists in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs — verify the route name before authoring; if neither exists the FR assertions become typed blockers). SQLite, mock connections, and stubbed repositories are explicitly forbidden anywhere in this suite.

SWARM/AccessKit INVARIANT (HARD): Each interop edge must be reachable purely via AccessKit author_ids registered by MT-041 (EditorActions) and MT-042 (KnowledgeGraph), plus the pane-specific ids registered by MT-066/067/068. OP-04 specifically proves an out-of-process agent path: it builds the egui widget tree with egui_kittest, looks up each target by author_id only (never by coordinates or label-scraping), simulates the AccessKit Activate/Press/SetValue action, and calls handshake_native::accessibility::flush_pending_updates() before asserting the resulting label/focus/value state.

FR-EVENT INVARIANT (HARD): Each scenario must, after driving its edge, assert that the EXPECTED Flight Recorder / EventLedger event(s) were emitted, by reading GET /api/flight_recorder and filtering for the edge's event kind (e.g. STAGE_ROUTE, STAGE_EMBED_BACK, CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND, ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED, LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED, LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP — verify the exact emitted kind strings against the upstream MT implementations; if a kind is not emitted, surface a typed blocker, do not weaken the assertion).

=== SCENARIO OP-01 — Stage interop (Pillar 17): route-to-Stage then embed-back round-trip ===
Proves the Stage edge end-to-end. Steps: (1) using the real PG fixture, create a workspace and a rich-text note with a known text range; (2) drive the 'route-to-stage' command via the shared command bus (the same command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs dispatch path MT-031/MT-046 use) so the selected range is routed to the Stage pane (Pillar 17) — reach the command via AccessKit author_id (EditorActions registry, MT-041) NOT by label; (3) assert the Stage pane received the routed selection (AccessKit author_id 'stage.selection.preview' label matches the routed text, after flush_pending_updates()); (4) drive 'embed-back': the Stage pane produces an embed that is inserted back into the originating note as an MT-014-style embed node carrying SHA-256 provenance of the routed payload; (5) save the note via the existing PUT /workspaces/{id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id}, reload via GET, and assert the block tree contains the embed node with a non-empty sha256 provenance field that matches the SHA-256 of the original routed bytes; (6) assert GET /api/flight_recorder contains a STAGE_ROUTE event followed by a STAGE_EMBED_BACK event for this workspace/doc, in order. Real PG only; no mocks.

=== SCENARIO OP-02 — Calendar interop (Pillar 2): daily-note <-> CalendarEvent binding + ActivitySpan correlation ===
Proves the Calendar edge end-to-end. Steps: (1) create a daily note for a fixed date via the existing knowledge-documents API; (2) drive the daily-note<->CalendarEvent binding (MT-067) so the daily note is bound to a real CalendarEvent row in PG (create the event via the existing calendar API or the binding command, NOT a direct mock); (3) assert the binding persists: reload the daily note and assert it carries the CalendarEvent id, and GET the CalendarEvent and assert it back-references the daily note doc_id; (4) generate an ActivitySpan correlated to that day (edits to documents on that date should correlate to an ActivitySpan); query the correlation and assert it returns the set of edited documents for the bound day (i.e. ActivitySpan correlation returns the edited documents); (5) assert GET /api/flight_recorder contains CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND and ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED events for the workspace/day. Reach the binding command via AccessKit id (swarm path). Real PG / real EventLedger only.

=== SCENARIO OP-03 — Locus interop (Pillar 6): locus:// cross-reference resolve + reverse lookup ===
Proves the Locus edge end-to-end. Steps: (1) create a WP/MT-style governed entity addressable by a locus:// URI (reuse the Locus cross-reference layer from MT-066; if Locus addressing requires a seeded governance entity, seed it via the existing API, not a mock); (2) in a note, insert a locus:// reference (e.g. locus://wp/WP-KERNEL-012/mt/MT-066) as a cross-reference node; save the note; (3) resolve the reference: drive the resolve path (via AccessKit id for the swarm path) and assert it resolves to the correct WP/MT target (returns the target's stable id and a resolvable title/anchor); (4) reverse lookup: query the Locus reverse-index for that WP/MT and assert it lists the referencing document(s) — i.e. the note created in step 2 appears in the reverse lookup result; (5) assert GET /api/flight_recorder contains LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED and LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP events for the workspace. Real PG only; the reverse index must be the real persisted index, not an in-memory map built by the test.

=== SCENARIO OP-04 — Swarm path: out-of-process agent drives each interop edge purely via AccessKit ids ===
Proves all three edges (Stage, Calendar, Locus) are addressable by an out-of-process agent with zero screen-reading. Steps: (1) build the full editor widget tree with egui_kittest; (2) for each of OP-01/OP-02/OP-03, look up the trigger ONLY by AccessKit author_id (from the MT-041 EditorActions registry and MT-042 KnowledgeGraph registry, plus pane ids from MT-066/067/068) — never by coordinates or by scraping visible labels; (3) simulate the AccessKit action (Activate / Press / SetValue) on that node; (4) call handshake_native::accessibility::flush_pending_updates(); (5) assert the post-action AccessKit tree exposes the expected result node (e.g. 'stage.selection.preview' updated, the calendar-binding indicator present, the locus-resolved chip present) — all read from the AccessKit tree, proving a no-vision agent can both DRIVE and VERIFY the edge; (6) assert each driven edge emitted its FR event (same kinds as OP-01..OP-03). This is the swarm-parity guarantee (HBR-SWARM): every other-pillar interop edge is operable headless via stable ids.

MANIFEST (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/other_pillar_interop_manifest.json): one entry per scenario with fields { scenario_id, edge, pillar, description, surfaces_involved: [], backend_apis_called: [], accesskit_ids: [], expected_fr_events: [], proof_fn, status }. 4 entries total (OP-01..OP-04). The test runner updates status to PASS / FAIL / BLOCKED. A BLOCKED entry must carry a typed blocker string naming the exact missing API, AccessKit id, or FR event kind.

STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Reuse the workspace + real-PostgreSQL fixture builder and DropGuard cleanup from MT-044/MT-045/MT-046. Do not create a second fixture; do not point it at SQLite.
2. Before authoring each scenario, VERIFY the backend route it needs exists in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs (Stage route+embed-back, Calendar bind/event, Locus resolve/reverse, and GET /api/flight_recorder). For any missing route, write the scenario body to detect the gap and mark the manifest entry BLOCKED with a typed blocker — never mock around it, never rewrite the backend.
3. Before authoring each scenario, VERIFY the AccessKit author_ids it needs were registered by the upstream MT (MT-066/067/068 pane ids; MT-041 EditorActions; MT-042 KnowledgeGraph). If an id is missing, mark BLOCKED with blocker='accesskit_id_missing:<id>'.
4. For SHA-256 provenance (OP-01), reuse the MT-014 embed provenance helper (compute sha256 over the exact routed bytes) — assert the stored provenance equals the recomputed digest; do not assert a placeholder.
5. For FR-event assertions, read GET /api/flight_recorder once after the edge is driven, filter to this workspace, and assert the expected kind(s) appear in order. If the FR query is async, poll up to 5 attempts with 200ms sleep (1 s budget) before failing, mirroring MT-046 CTRL-6.
6. OP-04 must look up every target by author_id only and call handshake_native::accessibility::flush_pending_updates() before each assertion; document that this is a layout-level (not GPU-render-level) AccessKit proof, mirroring MT-046 CTRL-3.
7. Every scenario cleans up its PG rows in a DropGuard so the suite is idempotent across reruns.
8. Register the suite as [[test]] name='test_other_pillar_interop_proofs' harness=true in src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml.
9. NO app code under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/** is created or edited. NO backend code under src/backend/** is edited (bind existing APIs only). Gaps become typed blockers.
acceptance_criteria: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_other_pillar_interop_proofs.rs exists and registers the four proof functions OP-01..OP-04 recognized by the harness (cargo test -p handshake-native -- other_pillar_interop reports 4 test functions).

src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/other_pillar_interop_manifest.json contains exactly 4 entries (OP-01..OP-04) each with fields: scenario_id, edge, pillar, description, surfaces_involved, backend_apis_called, accesskit_ids, expected_fr_events, proof_fn, status.

OP-01 (Stage route-to-Stage + embed-back): passes against REAL PostgreSQL — after route-to-Stage and embed-back, the reloaded note's block tree contains an MT-014-style embed node whose sha256 provenance equals the SHA-256 of the originally routed bytes; GET /api/flight_recorder contains STAGE_ROUTE then STAGE_EMBED_BACK in order.

OP-02 (Calendar daily-note<->CalendarEvent + ActivitySpan): passes against REAL PostgreSQL — the daily note is bound to a real CalendarEvent row (bidirectional reference persists across reload), and the ActivitySpan correlation for the bound day returns the set of edited documents; GET /api/flight_recorder contains CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND and ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED.

OP-03 (Locus resolve + reverse lookup): passes against REAL PostgreSQL — a locus:// reference in a note resolves to the correct WP/MT target, and the persisted reverse index lists the referencing note for that WP/MT; GET /api/flight_recorder contains LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED and LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP.

OP-04 (swarm path): passes — an out-of-process-style driver reaches and activates each interop edge PURELY via AccessKit author_ids (no coordinates, no label-scraping), and verifies each result by reading the AccessKit tree after flush_pending_updates(); each driven edge emits its expected FR event.

NO mocks, NO SQLite, NO stubbed repositories anywhere in the suite — all persistence is the real PostgreSQL fixture (reused from MT-044/045/046); proven by grep showing no sqlite/mock/in-memory-db usage in the test file.

NO files created or edited under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/** and NO files edited under src/backend/** — this is proof-only (verified by the diff touching only src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/** and src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml).

Any unmet dependency (missing backend route, missing AccessKit id, un-emitted FR event) is recorded as a TYPED BLOCKER in the manifest (status='BLOCKED', blocker='<reason>') — never mocked, stubbed, or silently skipped.
After a full passing run, other_pillar_interop_manifest.json has 0 FAIL entries; BLOCKED entries (if any) each carry a typed blocker string.
All scenarios are idempotent — a second run produces the same results and leaves no orphaned PG rows (DropGuard cleanup verified).
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native -- other_pillar_interop --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/other_pillar_run.txt && grep -c 'test other_pillar_op' /tmp/other_pillar_run.txt | grep -q '^4'

cat src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/other_pillar_interop_manifest.json | python -c "import sys,json; m=json.load(sys.stdin); fails=[e for e in m if e['status']=='FAIL']; print('FAIL:',len(fails)); assert len(fails)==0; print('entries:',len(m)); assert len(m)==4"
implementation_notes: Reuse the EXACT real-PostgreSQL workspace fixture builder and DropGuard from MT-044/MT-045/MT-046 — do not author a second fixture and do not substitute SQLite or an in-memory DB.

Upstream cross-WP dependency: this proof exercises WP-011 infrastructure already present under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ — the shared command/dispatch surface (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) and the AccessKit accessibility layer (accessibility/mod.rs, accessibility/live.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/snapshot.rs). Treat that WP-011 reuse as a prose dependency, not a depends_on entry.

OP-01 Stage: route-to-stage is the same shared command/dispatch edge MT-046 IC-05 exercises (the command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs dispatch path; the Stage pane lives in canvas_board.rs/Stage surface lineage). The Stage pane author_id is 'stage.selection.preview' (registered by MT-068/MT-034 lineage — verify it exists; if not, typed blocker). embed-back must produce an MT-014-style embed node; reuse the MT-014 embed provenance helper to compute and verify the SHA-256 over the routed bytes.

OP-02 Calendar: the daily-note<->CalendarEvent binding command and the ActivitySpan correlation query come from MT-067. Verify the calendar/event and activity-span routes exist in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs before asserting; if a route is missing, mark the scenario BLOCKED with the exact missing route name. Bind via the existing API; never insert a mock CalendarEvent.

OP-03 Locus: the locus:// resolve and reverse-lookup come from MT-066. The reverse index must be the real persisted index (PG-backed), not a HashMap built inside the test. Verify the resolve and reverse-lookup routes/commands exist; missing => typed blocker.

OP-04 swarm: look up every target ONLY by AccessKit author_id from the MT-041 EditorActions registry and MT-042 KnowledgeGraph registry plus the pane ids from MT-066/067/068. Never use coordinates or visible-label scraping. Call handshake_native::accessibility::flush_pending_updates() after each simulated action before asserting tree state. This is a layout-level AccessKit proof, not a GPU-render proof (mirror MT-046 CTRL-3).

FR events: read GET /api/flight_recorder (or GET /workspaces/{id}/events — verify the actual route name in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs). Filter to this workspace, assert expected kinds in order. Poll up to 5 attempts x 200ms (1 s budget) if the ledger query is async (mirror MT-046 CTRL-6). If the FR endpoint does not exist, the FR assertions become typed blockers.

Verify the exact emitted FR event kind strings against the upstream MT implementations (STAGE_ROUTE / STAGE_EMBED_BACK / CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND / ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED / LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED / LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP are the expected names; if an upstream MT emits a different kind, assert the real kind and note the discrepancy — do not weaken to a substring-only check).
Scenario function naming pattern: other_pillar_op{NN}_{snake_case_description}; the manifest proof_fn field must match exactly (mirror MT-046).
Register the suite under [[test]] name='test_other_pillar_interop_proofs' harness=true in src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml.

Every scenario cleans up PG rows in a DropGuard so reruns are idempotent. No app code under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/** and no backend edits under src/backend/** — proof only; gaps are typed blockers.

REALITY: STAGE/CALENDAR/LOCUS ROUTES ABSENT + NO MANAGED PG -> ALL-BLOCKED MANIFEST + GATE, DO NOT FAKE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-26, from MT-066/067/068 backend verification): the 3 edges this suite proves need backend HTTP routes that the earlier MTs VERIFIED ABSENT in handshake_core - STAGE has NO /stage/ routes (MT-066: route-to-stage is a BUS command, embed-back GET /stage/artifacts absent), CALENDAR has NO /calendar/ routes (MT-067: events + activity-spans absent), LOCUS has NO /locus/ HTTP routes (MT-068: kernel/governance data model only). AND there is NO managed PostgreSQL in this environment (every live-PG proof = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF). THEREFORE OP-01/OP-02/OP-03 CANNOT run green - and per the contract's OWN typed-blocker mandate (CTRL-4/8 + 'record as TYPED BLOCKER status=BLOCKED rather than mocked/stubbed/skipped') the suite MUST: (1) WRITE the 4 scenarios + the manifest, structurally correct, reusing the MT-044/045/046 PG fixture PATTERN; (2) GATE the live scenarios #[ignore] + #[cfg(feature="integration")] (NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF); (3) mark the manifest entries OP-01/OP-02/OP-03 status='BLOCKED' with typed blockers: blocker='missing_api:POST /stage/route + GET /stage/artifacts (Pillar 17, MT-066)', 'missing_api:/calendar/events + /calendar/activity-spans (Pillar 2, MT-067)', 'missing_api:/locus/resolve + /locus/reverse (Pillar 6, MT-068)' + 'no_managed_postgres'. NEVER mock/SQLite/in-memory (CTRL-1, RISK-1) NEVER edit src/backend (CTRL-8, RISK-8). The suite is the regression guard - goes green UNCHANGED when the backend packets expose the routes + a managed PG is available.

OP-04 SWARM-AccessKit REACHABILITY IS PROVABLE NOW (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): OP-04 (the out-of-process agent path) is the ONE scenario with a provable-NOW half - the AccessKit author_ids exist (stage-pane/stage-capture-embed-back MT-066, daily-journal-* MT-067, locus-ref-chip-* MT-068, editor.* MT-041, graph MT-042). Prove NON-ignored: build the editor widget tree with egui_kittest, look up each interop trigger PURELY by author_id (NO coordinates/label-scraping, CTRL-5), assert each id is present + STABLE across two re-queries + the AccessKit Activate/SetValue dispatch reaches the node, calling handshake_native::accessibility::flush_pending_updates() before asserting (layout-level proof, mirror MT-046 CTRL-3). The LIVE backend DRIVE of each edge (the half that needs the absent routes + PG) stays GATED. So OP-04's swarm-reachability/id-stability = PROVABLE NOW; OP-04's live edge-drive + FR-event = BLOCKED/gated. SEQUENCING NOTE: MT-074 reuses the MT-044/045/046 real-PG fixture, but 044-046 are DEFERRED-to-end proof capstones (same all-BLOCKED shape) - so reference the shared fixture builder (or a minimal shared helper) but keep everything gated; do NOT author a 2nd fixture (CTRL-1) and do NOT point at SQLite.

WHAT IS PROVABLE NOW vs GATED + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): PROVABLE NOW (non-ignored): cargo build --tests compiles the suite 0-warning; the manifest has exactly 4 entries (OP-01..04) with all required fields; OP-04 swarm AccessKit id-reachability + stability (the ids from MT-066/067/068/041/042 exist + are addressable headless); the FR-route name resolved (verify GET /api/flight_recorder vs /workspaces/{id}/events in api/mod.rs - if absent, that too is a blocker). GATED #[ignore]+integration (NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF + the absent routes): OP-01 Stage route+embed-back+SHA-256 provenance, OP-02 Calendar bind+activity-span, OP-03 Locus resolve+reverse, OP-04 live edge-drive + FR-event. PROOF-ONLY: ONLY test_other_pillar_interop_proofs.rs + other_pillar_interop_manifest.json + Cargo.toml [[test]] change - NO src/frontend/src or src/backend edit, NO SQLite (CTRL-8). DropGuard cleanup so reruns idempotent (CTRL-9). The all-BLOCKED manifest is the precise backend-packet backlog for the next WP. full suite -j 2 (serialize); clippy clean.
binds_backend_api: Stage route + embed-back endpoints (existing) — route a selection to Stage and insert the produced embed back into the note; verify exact route names in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs (OP-01).
PUT /workspaces/{workspace_id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id} — save the note carrying the embed-back / locus reference (OP-01, OP-03).
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/knowledge/documents/{doc_id} — reload to assert persisted embed/provenance and reference nodes (OP-01, OP-03).
Calendar event + daily-note binding endpoints (existing) — bind daily note to CalendarEvent and read the bidirectional reference (OP-02); verify exact route names.
ActivitySpan correlation endpoint (existing) — query the correlation for the bound day and assert it returns edited documents (OP-02).
Locus resolve + reverse-lookup endpoints (existing) — resolve a locus:// reference to its WP/MT target and list referencing documents (OP-03); verify exact route names.

GET /api/flight_recorder — assert the expected interop FR events were emitted (STAGE_ROUTE, STAGE_EMBED_BACK, CALENDAR_EVENT_BOUND, ACTIVITY_SPAN_CORRELATED, LOCUS_REF_RESOLVED, LOCUS_REVERSE_LOOKUP) for all four scenarios; verify whether the route is /api/flight_recorder or /workspaces/{id}/events before authoring.

risks: RISK-1: The proof uses mocks or an in-memory/SQLite database instead of real PostgreSQL, so the interop edges 'pass' without ever touching the real persistence layer — the proof would not catch a broken PG-backed reverse index or a missing CalendarEvent row.

RISK-2: Surfaces do not actually share state — each scenario calls an independent backend endpoint that happens to return the same data, so route-to-Stage / embed-back appears to work even if the shared command bus is broken (same failure mode as MT-046 RISK-1).
RISK-3: embed-back provenance is asserted shallowly (non-empty) but not recomputed, so a wrong or placeholder SHA-256 passes; the provenance guarantee is not actually proven.

RISK-4: Required backend routes (Stage embed-back, Calendar binding, ActivitySpan correlation, Locus reverse-lookup) or the GET /api/flight_recorder endpoint do not yet exist, and the author mocks around the gap instead of recording a typed blocker — hiding real interop debt.

RISK-5: AccessKit ids needed for the swarm path (OP-04, and the per-edge swarm lookups) were never registered by MT-041/MT-042/MT-066/067/068, so OP-04 falls back to coordinates or label-scraping and the no-vision agent guarantee is not actually proven.
RISK-6: FR-event assertions use a loose substring match or skip ordering, so the wrong event kind (or no event at all) passes; the EventLedger interop claim is not proven.
RISK-7: FR query / search indexing is async and the test reads the ledger before the events are visible, causing a flaky false FAIL on fast machines (or a false PASS if it asserts emptiness).
RISK-8: The proof MT silently grows scope by editing src/frontend/handshake_native/src/** or src/backend/** to make a scenario pass, violating the proof-only and no-backend-rewrite invariants.
RISK-9: Scenarios leave orphaned PG rows (no DropGuard), so a second run collides on unique constraints and reports a false FAIL, masking real regressions.
minimum_controls: CTRL-1 (RISK-1): All scenarios MUST use the real-PostgreSQL fixture reused from MT-044/045/046. Add a proof_target grep asserting the test file contains no sqlite/mock/in-memory-db usage; fail the MT if it does.

CTRL-2 (RISK-2): OP-01 and OP-04 must drive route-to-Stage through the SAME shared command/dispatch path the editors use (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) and assert the dispatch count incremented, not merely that the Stage pane shows matching text (mirror MT-046 CTRL-1).
CTRL-3 (RISK-3): OP-01 must recompute the SHA-256 of the exact routed bytes with the MT-014 provenance helper and assert equality with the stored embed provenance field — never assert non-empty only.

CTRL-4 (RISK-4): Before authoring each scenario, verify the needed route exists in src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/mod.rs and GET /api/flight_recorder (or /workspaces/{id}/events) exists. If missing, set the manifest entry status='BLOCKED' with a typed blocker naming the exact missing route — do NOT mock, do NOT rewrite the backend.

CTRL-5 (RISK-5): OP-04 must look up every target by AccessKit author_id only (no coordinates, no label-scraping) and verify results by reading the AccessKit tree after flush_pending_updates(). If a required author_id is not registered upstream, set status='BLOCKED', blocker='accesskit_id_missing:<id>' — do NOT fall back to coordinates.

CTRL-6 (RISK-6): FR-event assertions must check the exact emitted kind string AND the relative order of the two events per edge; verify the real kind strings against the upstream MT implementations rather than guessing.

CTRL-7 (RISK-7): FR-ledger reads must poll up to 5 attempts with 200ms sleep (1 s budget) before asserting, to tolerate async ledger writes (mirror MT-046 CTRL-6); after the budget, fail with 'flight_recorder_not_ready', never a trivially passing assertion.

CTRL-8 (RISK-8): The diff must touch ONLY src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/** and src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml. Any change under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/** or src/backend/** fails the MT; needed product/backend behavior that is missing is a typed blocker, not an edit.

CTRL-9 (RISK-9): Every scenario wraps its created PG rows in a DropGuard that deletes them on completion; a second full run must produce identical results with no unique-constraint collisions (idempotency proof_target).

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-066/067/068 backend verification (Stage/Calendar/Locus HTTP routes ALL absent) + no managed PG + MT-065 proof-capstone precedent + MT-044/045/046 PG fixture (deferred capstones) + MT-041/042 AccessKit ids
corrections: Stage/Calendar/Locus routes verified ABSENT (MT-066/067/068) + no managed PG -> OP-01/02/03 BLOCKED (typed missing_api blockers in the manifest) + #[ignore]/integration gate; do NOT mock/SQLite/backend-edit (the contract's own CTRL-4/8 mandate)
OP-04 swarm AccessKit REACHABILITY provable NOW (ids exist from MT-066/067/068/041/042 - lookup-by-author_id + stability + dispatch headless); live edge-drive + FR-event gated
reuse the MT-044/045/046 PG fixture pattern (those are deferred-to-end capstones, same all-BLOCKED shape - reference the shared fixture, no 2nd fixture, no SQLite); verify FR route name (api/mod.rs)
proof-only (only the test + manifest + Cargo.toml); the all-BLOCKED manifest = the precise backend-packet backlog; DropGuard idempotent; -j 2

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_1a253e5e (impl + 3-lens panel; ALL PASS - no harden)
commits: b914d631 feat MT-074 E10 other-pillar interop proof suite (Stage/Calendar/Locus); OP-04 swarm AccessKit proven, OP-01..03 live halves typed-blocked + gated
